creator: bugs-freebsd-org916@ketas.si.pri.ee
text: this doesn't seem to be actual fbsd ports error. but what is it? is it something that fbsd patch introduced to any tools those use, eg auto* maybe? or is it upstream? or a stray issue running them in fbsd? but why did it disappear? since i tried to get the error back by downgrading them. but downgrade somehow fixed something. something in fs i guess. since env didn't change. no config or tools did change. nothing changed. sadly i don't have full fs snapshots but i do have pkg db dump before (fails) and after event (ok). if this is stray issue, it's very weird one. maybe it's specific to portupgrade, but how portmaster fails then? the error is also from gmake and it's within upstream code apparently. oh i don't know. would anyone suggest anything? i diffed the two pkg dumps but couldn't find anything. or i don't know what to look. pkg db should perfectly reflect any file, lib and their relations, dep, etc on disk. i clearly changed something and now i can't get it back. maybe pkg bug even? i guess it won't show up with poudriere but what's even happening? i mean it's not question whether anyone should still use portupgrade in 2025 but why does this error happen. i don't like random bugs experienced by multiple users. i think i should have better fbsd lab where i can actually play with things like this, repeatedly, and find out the causes
